Real engagement · Factor_ · 2017–2018 · CMO role

A DTC meal-kit hyper-growth year that ended in a HelloFresh acquisition.

A Treetop fractional CMO engagement at Factor_ — the prepared-meal DTC brand that grew into one of the fastest-growing companies in America during the engagement window, named to the INC 5000 list in 2017 and 2018, and ultimately acquired by HelloFresh. The work: rebuild paid acquisition from $0 in spend to $4K+/day, cut blended CAC nearly in half, and lift lifecycle email revenue by an order of magnitude.

66%
Revenue growth in six months, driving the trajectory that led to HelloFresh acquisition
INC 5000
2017 + 2018
$0 → $4K+/day
paid spend scaled
Acquired
by HelloFresh

Named engagement, published per Bill Colbert's public career record. Specific revenue figures aggregated for confidentiality; growth multiples, CAC numbers, and other operational metrics reflect actual reported results.

By Bill Colbert · Founder, Treetop Growth Strategy
Published May 2026 · More case studies
The starting situation

May 2017: a credible DTC product, an unproven acquisition motion

Company: Factor_ — prepared, ready-to-eat meal delivery DTC brand. Real product credibility; strong early customer base; a category (prepared meals as DTC subscription) that was rapidly proving out across the broader meal-kit landscape.

The constraint: Paid acquisition was effectively unbuilt — the company hadn't yet cracked the meta-ads creative + funnel architecture needed to scale customer acquisition at meal-kit-category economics. Lifecycle email marketing existed but was underused; meaningful prospect segments weren't being nurtured to conversion. The growth ceiling was the marketing infrastructure, not the product or the market.

What Treetop was asked to own: Chief Marketing Officer role for the engagement. Reporting directly to the CEO. Own all marketing media investment, customer acquisition, lifecycle marketing, and the team that executed it. Build the acquisition engine that would let the company capture the demand the product had already earned.

What we built

The acquisition engine

Paid social acquisition (Meta)

  • Scaled spend from $0 to $4K+/day over the engagement window. A multi-tier funnel structure separating top-of-funnel prospecting, mid-funnel education, and bottom-of-funnel conversion creative — each layer creatively distinct and audience-targeted.
  • Blended CAC: $129 → $64. A 50% cut in customer acquisition cost while scaling spend by orders of magnitude — the opposite of what most operators see when they aggressively scale paid. The driver was creative architecture + funnel discipline, not just better targeting.
  • 775+ unique Meta ads tested. Continuous variant testing — copy hooks, video formats, imagery, gifs, audience permutations. A proprietary campaign setup structure that surfaced winning combinations faster than the standard A/B-test cadence.

Lifecycle email marketing

  • 1,100% revenue lift from email channels. Built automated lifecycle messaging flows segmented by prospect stage — first-visit, abandoned cart, post-purchase, re-engagement, win-back, loyalty. Each flow tuned to the audience's stage and intent rather than treated as undifferentiated email blasts.
  • Team mentorship contributing to a 34% lift in email-channel sales separately — the engagement also developed the junior team members' lifecycle marketing capability so the gains compounded after the campaign launches.

The compounding effect

  • 66% revenue growth in six months (Jul-Dec 2017). The combined effect of expanded paid acquisition + improved lifecycle email economics + the scaling team executing both with rising discipline.
  • INC 5000 recognition in 2017 and 2018. Two consecutive years on the list of America's fastest-growing private companies.
  • HelloFresh acquisition. The trajectory established in this engagement was a meaningful contributor to the company's eventual acquisition by HelloFresh — one of the most-recognized exits in the DTC food category.
Results

What changed in the engagement window

+66%
Revenue growth in 6 months
+1,100%
Email channel revenue lift
$129 → $64
Blended CAC (50% reduction)
$0 → $4K+/day
Paid acquisition spend scaled

Unique Meta ad variants tested
INC 5000
Listed 2017 + 2018
What we'd tell you about this pattern

What worked, and what this teaches about pre-acquisition DTC engagements

What worked

  • Scaling spend AND cutting CAC at the same time. The conventional wisdom is that CAC rises as you scale paid acquisition because you exhaust the highest-intent audiences. The Factor_ pattern proves the opposite is achievable when the creative + funnel architecture is doing the work — better creative + better funnel separation lets you scale into broader audiences without paying scale-tax on CAC. Most DTC operators give up on this too early.
  • Creative volume as a discipline, not an event. 775+ ads tested in an engagement window of less than a year. That's roughly 2-3 new creative variants going live every business day, sustained. The winning combinations couldn't have been predicted in advance; the testing volume surfaced them.
  • Lifecycle email as the second engine. Acquisition alone caps your growth at your ad budget. Lifecycle email economics are the multiplier — every acquired customer becomes a recurring revenue stream that doesn't require additional paid spend. The 1,100% email revenue lift was the structural addition that changed the unit economics of the whole business.
  • The acquisition story matters for exit value. Acquirers don't pay multiples on revenue; they pay multiples on the durability and scalability of the acquisition motion. The Factor_ engagement built the motion in a form that an acquirer could understand, validate, and absorb — which is materially different from just "growing fast."

If you're a DTC brand approaching a potential exit window

This pattern — fractional CMO engagement focused on building durable, scalable, exit-readable acquisition infrastructure — fits DTC brands at the inflection point where the next 12-18 months of growth will determine the strategic options available. The work isn't just "scale paid"; it's building the marketing infrastructure that makes the company more valuable to an acquirer than it would be without it.

For the standard fractional CMO engagement model: What is a fractional CMO. For the AI-native operating model that has evolved since 2018: AI-native fractional CMO.

DTC brand in the pre-acquisition / scale-up window?
